diff:
diff --git a/docs/EventSourceAndCounters.md b/docs/EventSourceAndCounters.md
+## HttpLogging structured logging fields
+RequestMethod, RequestPath, StatusCode, Duration fields documented with Splunk/ELK/KQL examples
diff --git a/src/Middleware/HttpLogging/src/HttpLoggingExtensions.cs b/src/Middleware/HttpLogging/src/HttpLoggingExtensions.cs
+    [LoggerMessage(10, LogLevel.Information,
+        "{RequestMethod} {RequestPath} responded {StatusCode} in {Duration}ms",
+        EventName = "RequestResponseSummary")]
+    public static partial void RequestResponseSummary(
+        this ILogger logger,
+        string requestMethod,
+        string requestPath,
+        int statusCode,
+        double duration);
diff --git a/src/Middleware/HttpLogging/src/HttpLoggingMiddleware.cs b/src/Middleware/HttpLogging/src/HttpLoggingMiddleware.cs
+            // Always emit the structured summary log with indexed fields for log aggregators.
+            _logger.RequestResponseSummary(
+                context.Request.Method,
+                context.Request.Path.Value ?? string.Empty,
+                context.Response.StatusCode,
+                logContext.GetDuration());

excluded_defs.name: Duration
excluded_defs.reason: read as adjacent context but not modified

exploration_log.aha_moment: The `[LoggerMessage]` attribute pattern in `HttpLoggingExtensions.cs` is the cleanest way to add named structured fields — it auto-generates a `LogDefine`-style method with proper named parameter binding.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have checked whether Event ID 10 was already used before picking it.

alternatives: Modify existing Duration/RequestLog calls to include extra fields
Add the summary log only in non-CombineLogs path
key_decisions.decision: Add a single RequestResponseSummary log entry unconditionally at the end of the try block, after all existing logging
key_decisions.reasoning: The summary should always be emitted for log aggregators regardless of the detailed CombineLogs mode; keeping it separate avoids coupling with the detailed logging paths

search_sequence.reasoning: Find the existing [LoggerMessage] patterns and Event IDs
search_sequence.result: Found IDs 1-9 in use; [LoggerMessage] attribute generates source-generated structured log methods; ID 10 is free

search_sequence.reasoning: Find where to add the structured summary call
search_sequence.result: End of inner try block before the outer finally is where Duration is logged; that's where the summary should go

search_sequence.reasoning: Task requires updating this doc file
search_sequence.result: File exists with EventSource/EventCounter guidance; appended a new HttpLogging section at the end

queries.justification: Rule compliance: pure domain concepts — 'HTTP request summary', 'structured logging fields', 'log aggregators', 'method path status duration'. Pre-implementation: a developer describes the feature request without knowing the classes.
queries.query_text: Add structured logging with named HTTP request summary fields so log aggregators can filter and query by method, path, status code, and duration without parsing log strings

queries.justification: Rule compliance: 'RequestResponseSummary', 'RequestMethod', 'RequestPath' are literal strings in the source code. Pre-implementation: searching for existing structured log events in the HttpLogging middleware.
Rule compliance: 4 exact symbol names from the changed code. Pre-implementation: developer searching for the logger extension methods and the middleware entry point.
Rule compliance: names HttpLoggingMiddleware with relationship word 'callers' and 'extension methods'. Pre-implementation: tracing what extension methods HttpLoggingMiddleware calls on the logger.
queries.query_text: Extension methods on ILogger called by HttpLoggingMiddleware, and callers of HttpLoggingExtensions log methods

queries.justification: Rule compliance: two file paths from the solution. Pre-implementation: developer navigates directly to the middleware and its logger extensions.
queries.query_text: src/Middleware/HttpLogging/src/ structured logging RequestMethod RequestPath StatusCode Duration

queries.justification: Rule compliance: mixes domain description with 3 exact symbols. Pre-implementation: developer writing the task as a natural query.
queries.query_text: Add structured LoggerMessage logging to HttpLoggingMiddleware with named fields RequestMethod, RequestPath, StatusCode, Duration for log aggregators

queries.justification: Rule compliance: 2 symbol names and 2 file paths. Pre-implementation: pinning both the middleware and the extensions file.
Rule compliance: contains symbol names and file paths. Pre-implementation: the full task description as a query.
queries.query_text: Add structured LoggerMessage.Define logging to HttpLoggingMiddleware with RequestMethod, RequestPath, StatusCode, Duration named fields and update docs/EventSourceAndCounters.md

solve_notes: Added a `[LoggerMessage]` partial method `RequestResponseSummary` (Event ID 10) to `HttpLoggingExtensions` with named structured fields: `RequestMethod`, `RequestPath`, `StatusCode`, `Duration`. Called it unconditionally at the end of `InvokeInternal`'s try block after all existing logging. Updated `docs/EventSourceAndCounters.md` with a documentation section for the new fields including usage examples for Splunk, ELK, and Azure Monitor.
task_text: `HttpLoggingMiddleware` in `src/Middleware/HttpLogging/src/HttpLoggingMiddleware.cs` logs request/response details as formatted strings. Add structured logging using `LoggerMessage.Define` with named fields (`RequestMethod`, `RequestPath`, `StatusCode`, `Duration`) so log aggregators can index and query individual fields without parsing. Also update `docs/EventSourceAndCounters.md` to document the new structured logging fields, their names, types, and usage examples for common log aggregation tools.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — see auditor report

thrash_preventing_defs.name: HttpLoggingExtensions
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: must understand the existing [LoggerMessage] patterns and next available Event ID before adding the new method

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the two methods that were edited. The thrash-preventing set adds HttpLoggingExtensions as a class-level read — an AI agent needs to see the full class to know what Event IDs are already used and confirm the partial keyword pattern before adding the new method.
